post.summary: Assign personal locker to locker user
post.description:
Assign a new Personal/Dynamic Locker (if Locker is NET/Smart) or Personal Locker Programming Card (if Locker is BLE) to locker user with specified details.
        <b> Personal/Dynamic Locker (NET & Smart): Assignment for this locker handled synchrnously.
        <b> Personal Locker Programming Card (BLE): Assigment for this locker handled asynchronously.

202.description: Accepted - The requesst was accepted for processing, but not yet completed. Locker assignment accepted for asynchronous processing because Locker is BLE.

204.description: No Content - The request was processed successfully. Locker assignment completed synchrnously because the Locker is NET/Smart.

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid request (e.g. missing required fields or malformed syntax).

401.description: Unauthorized - Authentication required or credentials invalid.

403.description: Forbidden - User does not have necessary permissions.

422.description: Unprocessable Entity - Valid request but business rule violation.

500.description: Internal Server Error - An unexpected error occurred while processing the request.

400.description: Bad Request - Invalid request (e.g. missing required fields or malformed syntax).

LockerActions.description:
Represent the available locker actions.

**NET & Smart Locker Actions**
* OpenLocker
* CloseLocker
* SetMaintenance
* RevertMaintenance
* EnableLocker
* DisableLocker
* QuitAlarm
* QuitAlarmAndEnableLocker

**BLE Locker Actions**
* OpenLocker
* SyncTime

LockerModes.description:
Represent the mode of locker. 
   
**NET & Smart Locker Modes**
* PersonalLocker
* DynamicLocker
* FreeLocker

**BLE Locker Modes**
* FreeLocker
* FreeLockerUID
* PersonalLockerProgrammingCard
* PersonalLockerExpiryDate

Authorizations.description: API Key authentication using X-API-KEY header
Authorizations.name: X-API-KEY

tags.name: Authorization Groups
tags.description: Authorization Groups represent collections of authorization rules that can be applied to multiple lockers or users at once. They simplify access control management by allowing you to define shared locker access rules, time plans, and limits for a group rather than individual authorizations.
tags.x-page-description: Organize and manage grouped authorization policies.

tags.name: Data Carrier Types
tags.description: Data Carrier Types define the configuration and format of credentials recognized by the system, such as UID formats, duration definitions, and hardware compatibility. This API provides control over supported card formats and activation states.
tags.x-page-description: Define and manage types of physical or digital access credentials.

tags.name: Data Carriers
tags.description: The Data Carriers API manages the access credentials (RFID cards, BLE tokens, NFC tags) that belong to locker users. Each data carrier acts as a unique identifier and can have associated authorizations, validity dates, and status flags such as enabled or disabled. Use this API to register, update credentials linked to locker users.
tags.x-page-description: Manage physical and digital credentials assigned to locker users.

tags.name: Duration Templates
tags.description: Duration Templates provide reusable definitions for time-based validity,  such as fixed dates, date ranges, or rolling time spans. These templates      are commonly used by data carrier types to standardize access durations.

tags.name: Locker Areas
tags.description: Locker Areas represent a hierarchical structure used to organize lockers      by logical grouping. Areas can contain child areas      and locker groups, making it easier to filter, manage, and assign lockers      in large installations.

tags.name: Lockers
tags.description: The Lockers API provides operations to list, search, and manage lockers in the system. Administrators can check locker availability, view open/closed or maintenance states, and manage locker assignments to locker users or authorizations.
tags.x-page-description: Manage physical lockers, configurations, and access state.

tags.name: Locker Users
tags.description: The Locker Users API manages individuals who are assigned lockers in the system. It supports creating and updating user records, linking them with authorization groups, and associating data carriers (RFID cards, tags, etc.) that are used for locker access.
tags.x-page-description: Manage locker users and their associated lockers and data carriers.

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: The Webhooks API allows external systems to receive notifications for key events such as locker activity, authorization updates, or data carrier changes. It enables real-time integrations and automation by sending event payloads to registered URLs.
tags.x-page-description: Subscribe to locker, authorization, and user activity events.
